## Preserved prior README notes

<!-- PRESERVED PRIOR README START -->
# normalization

This folder explains how raw extracted model-response fields were
normalized into canonical public fields used by scoring and analytics.

Normalization is not the same as scoring. Normalization maps free-form
or inconsistent response text into stable labels, flags, and method
classes. Scoring then compares those normalized/extracted fields to the
answer key in `../../7.scoring/answer-key/`.

## Subfolders

- `guides/`: per-surface normalization guides. These explain the field
  families and extraction/normalization rules for Schema A, Schema A New
  System, Schema B, Test 01, and Test 04.
- `method-maps/`: shared method-label dictionaries and renaming maps.
  These keep method-class naming stable across prompts, controls, and
  analytics.
- `overrides/`: active manual-review ledgers. These are the rows where
  a mechanical method-class rule was not sufficient and a public
  reviewer-readable adjudication record is needed.

## Most Important Provenance File

`overrides/schema_a_method_review_overrides.csv` is the active Schema A
manual-review ledger. It records reviewed rows whose method-validity
decisions are already reflected in `../csv/schema-test-A-tests.csv`,
especially through `turn1_method_mathematical_validity`,
`turn1_method_correct_and_admissible`, and
`turn1_method_review_note`.

This file is public so reviewers can reproduce why the released Schema A
CSV differs from a purely mechanical method-class rule.

## Public Dataflow

```text
normalization/guides + normalization/method-maps + normalization/overrides
  -> 6.extracted-data/csv/*.csv normalized fields
  -> 7.scoring verdict derivation
  -> 8.analytics paper-number outputs
```

Archived internal normalization notes are not required for the public
pipeline and are intentionally not exported.
<!-- PRESERVED PRIOR README END -->
